steps.title: Identify the goal and the key data
steps.content: The task is to contrast meteorite falls with meteorite finds. The notes give two specific numbers: about 1,200 recorded falls and over 60,000 recorded finds. An effective contrast must put both numbers side by side using language that signals a difference — words like “while” or “but.” Any choice that gives only one number, or no numbers at all, fails the contrast requirement.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'A meteorite that was observed falling to Earth before being recovered is known as a meteorite fall; all others are known as meteorite finds.': ✗ Provides definitions only — no numbers, so no numerical contrast.
  • Choice B — 'Meteorites found on Earth are divided into two categories: meteorite falls and meteorite finds.': ✗ Mentions both categories but gives no numbers — cannot contrast quantities.
  • Choice C — 'There have been about 1,200 recorded meteorite falls, or meteorites observed falling to Earth.': ✗ Gives only the falls number (1,200) and explains the definition — no comparison to the 60,000 finds.
  • Choice D — 'While there have been only about 1,200 recorded meteorite falls, there have been over 60,000 meteorite finds.': ✓ Uses “while” to directly contrast both numbers, showing falls are far fewer than finds.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
